channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tipping next to 57 petty hall road, 4th June
item.description:
Discarded rubbish and furniture. Fire hazard and blocks entry to garden

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The road / junction is flooded again, 4th June
item.description:
The road/ junction is flooded again as this was reported on Tuesday 2/06/26 after the heavy rain fall over the last few days. The drainage at the junction requires attention and made safe for road users and pedestrians
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pinnacle Hill, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sign defaced., 4th June
item.description:
The “en” of Bellenden has been painted over to comic effect.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 55, Bellenden Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Pot whole and litter, 4th June
item.description:
Screws and sharp object have left me with 3 punctures on my car.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Morpeth Grove, Hackney, London

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 4th June
item.description:
I have been out all day and went I got home only to find rubbish sacked up against the wall.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hassett Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Construction equipment, 4th June
item.description:
Construction equipment and excavation debris is being stored outside Deverill Court. No permission was sought and no notice given. Consequently essential tree pruning works have had to be postponed. This is in addition to the inconvenience caused to residents and visitors, which is maximised due to the number of residents in the block. Please have them relocate the equipment without delay

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Avenue Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.title: It’s Dead, 4th June
item.description:
The dead tree planted outside 8 Blackbrook Lane is still dead, it has no leaves and never has done. It’s now leaning over ans maybe a hazard if it continues to do so.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackbrook Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 4th June
item.description:
Found dead fox by pavement behind salt bin

item.title: Fly tipping of house hold furniture - bed side draws, 4th June
item.description:
Fly tipping of house hold furniture - bed side draws as in attached photo.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rowhill Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Furniture left on street, 4th June
item.description:
Chest of drawers left on the pavement at the end of Tyler St.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tyler Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking signs missing, 4th June
item.description:
This is on behalf of residents of Gylcote Close SE5 to raise a concern regarding the current parking arrangements within the close. For several years, the CPZ entry sign at the entrance to the close has been missing, along with most of the other parking signs that previously existed. As a result, Gylcote Close has become widely known locally as an area where people can park without restriction. This is now having a noticeable impact on residents. Vehicles from surrounding streets are regularly left in the close for extended periods, and it is becoming increasingly difficult for residents to park near their homes. It is not uncommon to see multiple vehicles belonging to the same non-resident households parked here. The current arrangement appears to be failing both residents and Southwark. Many residents have stopped purchasing parking permits because the restrictions are unclear and non-residents are routinely parking in the close without consequence. This must also be reducing permit revenue for the council. I would ask that Southwark review Gylcote Close and consider reinstating the missing signage, reviewing enforcement, and potentially extending the current restriction period beyond the existing 12pm-2pm window. We believe this would significantly improve parking availability for residents and increase compliance with the permit scheme. I would be grateful if this could be reviewed and if you could advise whether any action is planned. Kind regards, Gylcote Close Resident
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gylcote Close, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in front of bike bunkers, 4th June
item.description:
Please remove thx

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72, Vestry Road, Camberwell, Greater London

item.title: Untaxed vehicle, 4th June
item.description:
Untaxed car frequently parked on the side of the road, driven illegally most days. Parked there throughout the day. Tax expired feb 2026
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chilver Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: House hold effects dumped on pavement, 4th June
item.description:
House hold effects and other rubbish is regularly dumped at this location. Needs cameras to capture perpetrators, just collectingx whilst grateful - only reinforces the behaviour.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sydner Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cobourg Road - please remove the bin too!, 4th June
item.description:
Huge dump of household furniture and waste. This is a big problem already on the road and has been made much worse with the arrival of this big bin. Please remove the bin and all this waste asap. Whenever waste is left, more is dumped. Please please remove this bin to stop encouraging this behaviour. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cobourg Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
